name: OpenHAB Smart Home en

meta.description: OpenHAB Smart Home Integration (v0.4.3, englisch): Steuerung und Abfrage von Items inkl. historischer Datenaggregation. Aktuelle Hauptversion mit Beitrag von mr8ungle (Persistence-Feature).

content:
"""
title: OpenHAB Smart Home Tool
author: Torsten Feustel
contributor: mr8ungle (persistence feature base)
funding_url: https://github.com/torfeu/openhab-ai-integration
version: 0.4.3
required_open_webui_version: 0.3.9

OpenHAB Smart Home Integration with Enhanced Error Handling,
Persistence Queries, LLM-driven Aggregation and Statistics.
"""

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any, List, Set, Tuple
import requests
import json
import re
import statistics
from collections import defaultdict
import time
import logging
import threading
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
from datetime import datetime
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo

# Module logger — let host application (Open WebUI) configure handlers/levels
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class OpenHABHelper:
    """
    Helper class for OpenHAB operations with improved error handling
    """

    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # Construction from Open WebUI valves
    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # These factories live on the helper itself rather than in Tools so
    # that the Tools class contains *only* LLM-callable methods. Some LLMs
    # ignore the underscore-convention and try to invoke private helpers
    # — keeping Tools free of any private logic prevents that.

    @staticmethod
    def _filtered_types_from(valves) -> List[str]:
        """Builds the type-filter list from Open WebUI valves."""
        filtered_types = []
        if valves.filter_group_items:
            filtered_types.append("Group")
        if valves.custom_filtered_types:
            filtered_types.extend(
                t.strip() for t in valves.custom_filtered_types.split(",") if t.strip()
            )
        return filtered_types

    @staticmethod
    def _cache_services_from(valves) -> List[str]:
        """Builds the persistence-service list from Open WebUI valves."""
        return [
            s.strip() for s in valves.persistence_cache_services.split(",") if s.strip()
        ]

    @staticmethod
    def _signature(valves, filtered_types: List[str], cache_services: List[str]):
        """Identity tuple covering every valve the helper depends on.

        Any future valve added to __init__ must be reflected here so that a
        config change in Open WebUI causes the helper to be rebuilt with
        fresh caches.
        """
        return (
            valves.openhab_host,
            valves.openhab_port,
            valves.openhab_api_token,
            valves.cache_ttl,
            valves.enable_item_filter,
            tuple(filtered_types),
            valves.timezone,
            valves.enable_persistence_cache,
            tuple(cache_services),
        )

    @classmethod
    def from_valves(
        cls, valves, current: Optional["OpenHABHelper"] = None
    ) -> "OpenHABHelper":
        """Returns a helper matching the current valve configuration.

        If `current` is supplied and its config signature matches the valves,
        it is returned unchanged (cache preserved). Otherwise a fresh helper
        with empty caches is built.
        """
        filtered_types = cls._filtered_types_from(valves)
        cache_services = cls._cache_services_from(valves)
        sig = cls._signature(valves, filtered_types, cache_services)

        if current is not None and getattr(current, "_config_sig", None) == sig:
            return current

        # Config changed → a fresh helper with empty caches is built below.
        # Close the outgoing helper's HTTP session first so its pooled
        # connections don't leak across repeated valve changes.
        if current is not None:
            try:
                current.session.close()
            except Exception:
                pass

        helper = cls(
            host=valves.openhab_host,
            port=valves.openhab_port,
            api_token=valves.openhab_api_token,
            cache_ttl=valves.cache_ttl,
            filter_enabled=valves.enable_item_filter,
            filtered_types=filtered_types,
            timezone_name=valves.timezone,
            persistence_cache_enabled=valves.enable_persistence_cache,
            persistence_cache_services=cache_services,
        )
        helper._config_sig = sig
        return helper

    def __init__(
        self,
        host: str,
        port: str,
        api_token: str = "",
        cache_ttl: int = 3600,
        filter_enabled: bool = True,
        filtered_types: List[str] = None,
        timezone_name: str = "Europe/Berlin",
        persistence_cache_enabled: bool = True,
        persistence_cache_services: List[str] = None,
    ):
        self.host = host
        self.port = port
        self.api_token = api_token
        self.cache_ttl = cache_ttl
        self.vocabulary_cache = None
        self.vocabulary_last_update = None
        self.search_index = None
        self.filter_enabled = filter_enabled
        self.filtered_types = filtered_types or []
        self.last_load_error = None
        self.last_successful_load = None
        # Structural fingerprint of the last loaded item set (name/type/label/
        # category/tags). Used by check_items_changed() instead of
        # max(lastStateChange) so that constant state changes in a live
        # installation no longer trigger pointless vocabulary/index rebuilds.
        self.last_items_fingerprint: Optional[int] = None
        # Throttle for items-change detection inside get_vocabulary()
        # (Valve changes are handled by from_valves signature check, so this
        # only needs to catch OpenHAB-side item additions/renames.)
        self.last_items_check: Optional[float] = None
        self.items_check_interval: int = 60  # seconds
        try:
            self.tz = ZoneInfo(timezone_name)
        except Exception:
            self.tz = ZoneInfo("Europe/Berlin")
        self.persistence_cache_enabled = persistence_cache_enabled
        self.persistence_cache_services = persistence_cache_services or [
            "jdbc",
            "rrd4j",
        ]
        # Persistence availability cache: {item_name: {service_id: (first_str, last_str)}}
        self.persistence_cache: Dict[str, Dict[str, Tuple[str, str]]] = {}
        self.persistence_cache_time: Optional[float] = None
        self.persistence_cache_ttl: int = 3600
        self._persistence_refresh_lock = threading.Lock()

        # Shared HTTP session with connection pooling — major speedup for
        # the persistence cache rebuild (740 requests on 370 items × 2 services)
        self.session = requests.Session()
        adapter = requests.adapters.HTTPAdapter(pool_connections=20, pool_maxsize=20)
        self.session.mount("http://", adapter)
        self.session.mount("https://", adapter)

    def get_base_url(self) -> str:
        """Create OpenHAB Base URL"""
        return f"http://{self.host}:{self.port}/rest"

    def get_headers(self) -> Dict[str, str]:
        """HTTP Headers for OpenHAB API"""
        headers = {"Accept": "application/json"}
        if self.api_token:
            headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {self.api_token}"
        return headers

    def should_filter_item(self, item: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
        """Check if an item should be filtered"""
        if not self.filter_enabled:
            return False
        item_type = item.get("type", "")
        return item_type in self.filtered_types

    def is_controllable_item(self, item: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
        """Uses OpenHAB's readOnly flag for precise detection"""
        state_description = item.get("stateDescription", {})
        read_only = state_description.get("readOnly", False)

        if read_only:
            return False

        item_type = item.get("type", "")
        never_controllable = {"DateTime", "Location"}

        if item_type in never_controllable:
            return False

        if item_type == "Group":
            return False

        return True

    def get_possible_commands(self, item: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[str]:
        """Derives possible commands from item type"""
        if not self.is_controllable_item(item):
            return []

        item_type = item.get("type", "")

        command_mapping = {
            "Switch": ["on", "off", "toggle"],
            "Dimmer": ["on", "off", "0-100%"],
            "Color": ["on", "off", "color", "brightness"],
            "Rollershutter": ["up", "down", "0-100%"],
            "Number:Temperature": ["15-30°C"],
            "Number:Dimensionless": ["0-100"],
            "Number:ElectricCurrent": ["current value"],
            "Number:Power": ["power value"],
            "Number:Energy": ["energy value"],
            "Number:Pressure": ["pressure value"],
            "Number:Humidity": ["humidity value"],
            "Number": ["numeric value"],
            "Player": ["play", "pause", "stop"],
            "String": ["text"],
            "Contact": ["status"],
        }

        if "Number:" in item_type and item_type not in command_mapping:
            return ["numeric value"]

        return command_mapping.get(item_type, ["command"])

    def load_openhab_items(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Loads all OpenHAB items with improved error handling"""
        try:
            response = self.session.get(
                f"{self.get_base_url()}/items", headers=self.get_headers(), timeout=15
            )

            if response.status_code == 200:
                all_items = response.json()

                if not isinstance(all_items, list):
                    error_msg = (
                        f"Invalid response: Expected list, received {type(all_items)}"
                    )
                    logger.error(error_msg)
                    self.last_load_error = error_msg
                    return []

                if len(all_items) == 0:
                    logger.warning("OpenHAB returned 0 items - possibly a problem")
                    self.last_load_error = "No items received from OpenHAB"
                    return []

                if self.filter_enabled:
                    filtered_items = [
                        item for item in all_items if not self.should_filter_item(item)
                    ]
                    logger.info(
                        f"Filter active: {len(all_items)} items -> {len(filtered_items)} items "
                        f"(filtered: {self.filtered_types})"
                    )
                    all_items = filtered_items
                else:
                    logger.info(f"Filter disabled: {len(all_items)} items loaded")

                self.last_successful_load = time.time()
                self.last_load_error = None
                return all_items

            else:
                error_msg = (
                    f"OpenHAB API error {response.status_code}: {response.text[:200]}"
                )
                logger.error(error_msg)
                self.last_load_error = error_msg
                return []

        except requests.exceptions.Timeout:
            error_msg = f"Timeout connecting to OpenHAB ({self.host}:{self.port})"
            logger.error(error_msg)
            self.last_load_error = error_msg
            return []

        except requests.exceptions.ConnectionError:
            error_msg = f"Connection error to OpenHAB ({self.host}:{self.port})"
            logger.error(error_msg)
            self.last_load_error = error_msg
            return []

        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            error_msg = "OpenHAB returned invalid JSON"
            logger.error(error_msg)
            self.last_load_error = error_msg
            return []

        except Exception as e:
            error_msg = f"Unexpected error loading items: {str(e)}"
            logger.error(error_msg)
            self.last_load_error = error_msg
            return []

    def extract_vocabulary(self, items_list: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> Set[str]:
        """Automatically extracts keywords ONLY from active (non-filtered) items"""
        vocabulary = set()

        for item in items_list:
            if self.should_filter_item(item):
                continue

            name = item.get("name", "")
            if name:
                name_parts = name.lower().split("_")
                valid_parts = [
                    part for part in name_parts if len(part) > 2 and not part.isdigit()
                ]
                vocabulary.update(valid_parts)

            label = item.get("label", "")
            if label:
                words = re.split(r"[^a-zA-ZäöüÄÖÜß]+", label.lower())
                valid_words = [word for word in words if len(word) > 2]
                vocabulary.update(valid_words)

            state = str(item.get("state", "")).upper()
            if state in ["ON", "OFF", "OPEN", "CLOSED", "NULL", "UNDEF"]:
                vocabulary.add(state.lower())

            category = item.get("category", "")
            if category and len(category) > 2:
                vocabulary.add(category.lower())

            tags = item.get("tags", [])
            for tag in tags:
                if isinstance(tag, str) and len(tag) > 2:
                    vocabulary.add(tag.lower())

        # Inline filters above already enforce length > 2 and digit-check,
        # so no separate cleanup pass is needed.
        return vocabulary

    def build_search_index(
        self, items_list: List[Dict[str, Any]]
    ) -> Dict[str, List[Dict[str, Any]]]:
        """Creates an index for fast searching.

        Returns the freshly built index instead of mutating self.search_index
        in place, so get_vocabulary() can swap it in atomically. A concurrent
        search therefore always sees either the complete old index or the
        complete new one — never a half-populated defaultdict.
        """
        index: Dict[str, List[Dict[str, Any]]] = defaultdict(list)

        for item in items_list:
            if self.should_filter_item(item):
                continue

            name = item.get("name", "").lower()
            name_parts = name.split("_")
            for part in name_parts:
                if len(part) > 2:
                    index[part].append(item)

            label = item.get("label", "").lower()
            if label:
                words = re.split(r"[^a-zA-ZäöüÄÖÜß]+", label)
                for word in words:
                    if len(word) > 2:
                        index[word].append(item)

            category = item.get("category", "").lower()
            if category:
                index[category].append(item)

            for tag in item.get("tags", []):
                if isinstance(tag, str):
                    index[tag.lower()].append(item)

        return index

    def _items_fingerprint(self, items: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> int:
        """Order-independent hash of item *structure* — never of state.

        Vocabulary and search index depend solely on name/type/label/category/
        tags. Using a structural fingerprint (instead of max(lastStateChange))
        means that the constant stream of state changes in a busy installation
        no longer invalidates the cache: only genuine additions, removals,
        renames, relabels or tag changes trigger a rebuild.
        """
        return hash(
            frozenset(
                (
                    i.get("name", ""),
                    i.get("type", ""),
                    i.get("label", ""),
                    i.get("category", ""),
                    tuple(sorted(i.get("tags", []) or [])),
                )
                for i in items
            )
        )

    def check_items_changed(self, items: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> bool:
        """Checks whether the item *structure* changed since the last load.

        Compares a structural fingerprint (see _items_fingerprint), NOT
        lastStateChange — so live state churn does not force rebuilds.
        """
        if not items:
            return False

        fingerprint = self._items_fingerprint(items)

        if self.last_items_fingerprint is not None:
            if fingerprint != self.last_items_fingerprint:
                logger.info("Item structure changed - cache will be rebuilt")
                self.last_items_fingerprint = fingerprint
                return True

        self.last_items_fingerprint = fingerprint
        return False

    def get_vocabulary(self, force_reload: bool = False) -> Set[str]:
        """Returns the current vocabulary with intelligent smart caching.

        Caching strategy:
          - Valve changes (host, port, filter, ...) are handled by from_valves()
            which constructs a fresh helper with empty caches.
          - This method only needs to react to OpenHAB-side changes
            (new/renamed items) and TTL expiry.
          - To avoid hitting OpenHAB on every search, the items-change probe
            is throttled to once per items_check_interval seconds.
        """
        now = time.time()

        cache_age = None
        if self.vocabulary_last_update is not None:
            cache_age = now - self.vocabulary_last_update

        # Fast path: cache populated and not yet stale → maybe skip the probe
        probed_items: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None
        if (
            not force_reload
            and self.vocabulary_cache is not None
            and cache_age is not None
        ):
            # Throttle the items-change probe — don't re-fetch on every call
            time_since_check = (
                (now - self.last_items_check)
                if self.last_items_check is not None
                else float("inf")
            )
            need_probe = (
                time_since_check >= self.items_check_interval
                or cache_age > self.cache_ttl
            )

            if not need_probe:
                logger.debug(
                    f"Cache valid ({cache_age:.1f}s), next probe in "
                    f"{self.items_check_interval - time_since_check:.0f}s"
                )
                return self.vocabulary_cache

            probed_items = self.load_openhab_items()
            self.last_items_check = now

            if probed_items:
                items_changed = self.check_items_changed(probed_items)

                if not items_changed and cache_age <= self.cache_ttl:
                    logger.debug(f"Cache valid ({cache_age:.1f}s) and items unchanged")
                    return self.vocabulary_cache

                if items_changed:
                    logger.warning("Cache invalidated! Items changed.")
                    force_reload = True
                elif cache_age > self.cache_ttl:
                    logger.info(f"Cache expired ({cache_age:.1f}s) - reloading")
                    force_reload = True

        # Reuse already-loaded items from the probe; otherwise fetch now
        if probed_items is not None:
            items = probed_items
            logger.info("Reloading items... (reusing probe result)")
        else:
            logger.info("Reloading items...")
            items = self.load_openhab_items()
            self.last_items_check = now

        if not items and self.vocabulary_cache is not None:
            logger.warning("Items could not be loaded, using old cache")
            return self.vocabulary_cache
        elif not items:
            logger.error("Items could not be loaded and no cache available!")
            return set()

        self.check_items_changed(items)
        new_vocab = self.extract_vocabulary(items)
        new_index = self.build_search_index(items)
        # Atomic swap: a concurrent search sees the complete old state or the
        # complete new state, never a partially populated index.
        self.vocabulary_cache = new_vocab
        self.search_index = new_index
        self.vocabulary_last_update = now

        # Refresh persistence cache in background so search results have history info
        if self.persistence_cache_enabled and self.persistence_cache_services:
            t = threading.Thread(
                target=self.refresh_persistence_cache,
                args=(items, self.persistence_cache_services),
                daemon=True,
            )
            t.start()

        logger.info(f"Vocabulary updated: {len(self.vocabulary_cache)} words")
        return self.vocabulary_cache

    def normalize_umlauts(self, word: str) -> str:
        """Normalizes German umlauts for OpenHAB compatibility"""
        umlaut_map = {
            "ä": "ae",
            "ö": "oe",
            "ü": "ue",
            "Ä": "AE",
            "Ö": "OE",
            "Ü": "UE",
            "ß": "ss",
        }
        normalized = word
        for umlaut, replacement in umlaut_map.items():
            normalized = normalized.replace(umlaut, replacement)
        return normalized

    def search_items_and(self, query_words: List[str]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """AND search: Items must contain ALL query words"""
        if not self.search_index:
            return []

        query_words_lower = [word.lower() for word in query_words]

        item_sets = []
        for word in query_words_lower:
            if word in self.search_index:
                item_set = set()
                for item in self.search_index[word]:
                    if not self.should_filter_item(item):
                        item_set.add(item["name"])
                item_sets.append(item_set)
            else:
                return []

        if not item_sets:
            return []

        common_items = item_sets[0]
        for item_set in item_sets[1:]:
            common_items = common_items.intersection(item_set)

        # O(n) dedup via set lookup instead of O(n²) "any(r['name'] == ...)"
        seen: Set[str] = set()
        results = []
        for word in query_words_lower:
            for item in self.search_index[word]:
                name = item["name"]
                if (
                    name in common_items
                    and name not in seen
                    and not self.should_filter_item(item)
                ):
                    seen.add(name)
                    item_copy = item.copy()
                    item_copy["match_score"] = len(query_words)
                    results.append(item_copy)

        return results

    def search_items_or(self, query_words: List[str]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """OR search: Items must contain at least ONE query word"""
        if not self.search_index:
            return []

        query_words_lower = [word.lower() for word in query_words]
        results_dict = {}

        for word in query_words_lower:
            if word in self.search_index:
                for item in self.search_index[word]:
                    if self.should_filter_item(item):
                        continue

                    item_name = item["name"]
                    if item_name not in results_dict:
                        item_copy = item.copy()
                        item_copy["match_score"] = 1
                        item_copy["matched_words"] = [word]
                        results_dict[item_name] = item_copy
                    else:
                        if word not in results_dict[item_name]["matched_words"]:
                            results_dict[item_name]["match_score"] += 1
                            results_dict[item_name]["matched_words"].append(word)

        results = list(results_dict.values())
        results.sort(key=lambda x: x["match_score"], reverse=True)
        return results

    def validate_query(
        self, query_words: List[str], search_mode: str = "and"
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Validates the search query against the vocabulary"""
        if not query_words:
            return {"valid": False, "error": "Empty search query"}

        if len(query_words) > 10:
            return {"valid": False, "error": "Too many search terms (max 10)"}

        vocabulary = self.get_vocabulary()

        if not vocabulary:
            return {
                "valid": False,
                "error": f"Vocabulary could not be loaded: {self.last_load_error}",
                "complete_vocabulary": [],
            }

        valid_words = [w for w in query_words if w.lower() in vocabulary]
        invalid_words = [w for w in query_words if w.lower() not in vocabulary]

        if invalid_words:
            vocabulary_list = sorted(list(vocabulary))

            if search_mode.lower() == "and":
                error_msg = f"AND search stopped!\n\nInvalid keywords: {', '.join(invalid_words)}\n\nAND search requires ALL terms in the vocabulary."
            else:
                error_msg = f"Invalid keywords found: {', '.join(invalid_words)}"

            error_msg += (
                f"\n\nPlease use only these {len(vocabulary_list)} available keywords:"
            )

            return {
                "valid": False,
                "error": error_msg,
                "invalid_words": invalid_words,
                "valid_words": valid_words,
                "complete_vocabulary": vocabulary_list,
                "recommend_vocabulary_check": True,
            }

        if not valid_words:
            vocabulary_list = sorted(list(vocabulary))
            return {
                "valid": False,
                "error": "No valid search terms found",
                "complete_vocabulary": vocabulary_list,
                "recommend_vocabulary_check": True,
            }

        return {
            "valid": True,
            "valid_words": valid_words,
            "invalid_words": invalid_words,
        }

    def format_search_results(
        self, results: List[Dict[str, Any]], mode: str, max_results: int = 50
    ) -> str:
        """Formats search results WITH controllability information without emojis"""
        if not results:
            msg = "No results found."
            if mode.lower() == "and":
                msg += "\n\nTip: AND mode requires ALL terms to match. Try 'or' mode for broader results."
            msg += "\nUse get_vocabulary_list() to see all available keywords."
            return msg

        filter_info = ""
        if self.filter_enabled and self.filtered_types:
            filter_info = f" | Filter: {', '.join(self.filtered_types)} excluded"

        controllable_count = sum(
            1 for item in results if self.is_controllable_item(item)
        )
        readonly_count = len(results) - controllable_count

        output = (
            f"Search results ({mode.upper()} mode, {len(results)} found{filter_info})\n"
        )
        output += (
            f"{controllable_count} controllable | {readonly_count} status only\n\n"
        )

        for item in results[:max_results]:
            name = item.get("name", "Unknown")
            label = item.get("label", name)
            state = item.get("state", "N/A")
            item_type = item.get("type", "Unknown")
            match_score = item.get("match_score", 0)

            control_indicator = (
                "[CONTROLLABLE]" if self.is_controllable_item(item) else "[STATUS]"
            )

            state_str = str(state)
            state_str = re.sub(r"(\d+\.\d{2})\d+", r"\1", state_str)

            output += f"{control_indicator} {label} ({item_type}) | {state_str} | Score: {match_score}\n"
            output += f"   {name}\n"
            persistence_line = self.get_persistence_line(name)
            if persistence_line:
                output += f"{persistence_line}\n"

        if len(results) > max_results:
            hidden = len(results) - max_results
            output += f"... and {hidden} more results\n"

        return output

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # PERSISTENCE AVAILABILITY CACHE
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _fetch_persistence_span(
        self, item_name: str, service_id: str, start_date: str = "2020-01-01T00:00:00"
    ) -> Optional[Tuple[str, str]]:
        """Fetches first and last timestamp for one item+service. Returns (first, last) or None."""
        try:
            r = self.session.get(
                f"{self.get_base_url()}/persistence/items/{item_name}",
                params={"serviceId": service_id, "starttime": start_date},
                headers=self.get_headers(),
                timeout=8,
            )
            if r.status_code != 200:
                return None
            data = r.json().get("data", [])
            if not data:
                return None
            fmt = "%Y-%m-%d"
            first_str = datetime.fromtimestamp(
                data[0]["time"] / 1000, tz=self.tz
            ).strftime(fmt)
            last_str = datetime.fromtimestamp(
                data[-1]["time"] / 1000, tz=self.tz
            ).strftime(fmt)
            return (first_str, last_str)
        except Exception:
            return None

    def refresh_persistence_cache(
        self,
        items_list: List[Dict[str, Any]],
        services: List[str] = None,
        max_workers: int = 20,
    ) -> None:
        """Queries all items×services in parallel and populates persistence_cache.
        No auth required: /persistence/items/{name} is accessible with user role.
        """
        with self._persistence_refresh_lock:
            if (
                self.persistence_cache_time is not None
                and (time.time() - self.persistence_cache_time)
                < self.persistence_cache_ttl
            ):
                return  # Still fresh

            services = services or ["jdbc", "rrd4j"]
            # Note: /persistence/items/{name} works without auth (user role)
            item_names = [i["name"] for i in items_list]

            # Known start dates per service (avoid scanning from year 2000)
            service_start = {
                "jdbc": "2020-01-01T00:00:00",
                "rrd4j": "2020-01-01T00:00:00",
            }

            def _check(args):
                name, svc = args
                return (
                    name,
                    svc,
                    self._fetch_persistence_span(
                        name,
                        svc,
                        start_date=service_start.get(svc, "2024-01-01T00:00:00"),
                    ),
                )

            tasks = [(name, svc) for name in item_names for svc in services]
            new_cache: Dict[str, Dict[str, Tuple[str, str]]] = {}

            logger.info(
                f"Persistence cache: querying {len(tasks)} item×service combos..."
            )
            t0 = time.time()
            with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_workers) as ex:
                for name, svc, span in ex.map(_check, tasks):
                    if span:
                        if name not in new_cache:
                            new_cache[name] = {}
                        new_cache[name][svc] = span

            self.persistence_cache = new_cache
            self.persistence_cache_time = time.time()
            elapsed = time.time() - t0
            logger.info(
                f"Persistence cache built: {len(new_cache)} items with data "
                f"({elapsed:.1f}s, {len(tasks)} requests)"
            )

    def get_persistence_line(self, item_name: str) -> str:
        """Returns a compact one-line string with persistence availability, e.g.:
        'History: jdbc 2026-05-19→2026-05-23 | rrd4j 2026-04-12→2026-05-23'
        Returns empty string if no data in any service and no cache available.
        Iterates the configured services so a custom valve setting is honored.
        """
        if not self.persistence_cache:
            return ""
        entry = self.persistence_cache.get(item_name, {})
        if not entry:
            return "   History: no history"
        parts = []
        for svc in self.persistence_cache_services:
            if svc in entry:
                first, last = entry[svc]
                parts.append(f"{svc} {first}→{last}")
        if not parts:
            return "   History: no history"
        return "   History: " + " | ".join(parts)

    def persistence_overview_text(self) -> str:
        """Human-readable persistence overview for the configured services.

        Returns an empty string when the availability cache is not (yet)
        populated. Counting lives here rather than in the Tools class so that
        Tools stays a pure LLM-facing surface.
        """
        if not self.persistence_cache:
            return ""
        parts = ["\n\nPersistence overview (for historical queries):"]
        for svc in self.persistence_cache_services:
            count = sum(1 for v in self.persistence_cache.values() if svc in v)
            parts.append(f"- {svc}: {count} items with time-series data")
        parts.append(
            "Historical data is shown per item in search results "
            "(search first, then query history)."
        )
        return "\n".join(parts)

    def send_command(self, device_name: str, command: str) -> bool:
        """Sends command to OpenHAB device"""
        try:
            headers = self.get_headers()
            headers["Content-Type"] = "text/plain"

            command_mapping = {
                "on": "ON",
                "off": "OFF",
                "toggle": "TOGGLE",
                "up": "UP",
                "down": "DOWN",
            }

            mapped_command = command_mapping.get(command.lower(), command)

            response = self.session.post(
                f"{self.get_base_url()}/items/{device_name}",
                data=mapped_command,
                headers=headers,
                timeout=10,
            )

            return response.status_code in [200, 202]

        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Error sending command: {e}")
            return False

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # PERSISTENCE / HISTORICAL DATA
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _ts_to_str(self, ts_ms: int) -> str:
        """Converts millisecond timestamp to local time ISO string (no UTC offset).
        Local time is used throughout to match the datetime format the LLM passes
        in starttime/endtime (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS without timezone suffix).
        """
        try:
            return datetime.fromtimestamp(ts_ms / 1000, tz=self.tz).strftime(
                "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"
            )
        except Exception:
            return str(ts_ms)

    def _fmt_state(self, state: Any) -> Any:
        """Normalizes a raw state value for LLM output.
        Numeric states are rounded to 4 decimals to strip floating-point
        representation noise ('13.620000000000001' -> '13.62') and to drop
        insignificant trailing zeros ('20.0' -> '20'). Non-numeric states
        (ON/OFF, OPEN/CLOSED, DateTime strings, ...) are passed through
        unchanged so switch/contact/datetime semantics are never altered.
        """
        try:
            v = round(float(state), 4)
        except (ValueError, TypeError):
            return state
        return str(int(v)) if v == int(v) else str(v)

    def _tz_label(self) -> str:
        """Human-readable timezone label with the current UTC offset, e.g.
        'Europe/Berlin (UTC+02:00)'. Every 'time' field in the result uses this
        local timezone and is rendered WITHOUT an offset suffix so it
        round-trips with the offset-less starttime/endtime the LLM passes in.
        """
        try:
            offset = datetime.now(self.tz).utcoffset()
            total_min = int(offset.total_seconds() // 60)
            sign = "+" if total_min >= 0 else "-"
            hh, mm = divmod(abs(total_min), 60)
            return f"{self.tz.key} (UTC{sign}{hh:02d}:{mm:02d})"
        except Exception:
            return str(getattr(self.tz, "key", "local"))

    def _compute_statistics(self, data: List[Dict]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Computes statistics from the FULL dataset before any truncation/aggregation.
        Returns min, max, average, median with timestamps.
        """
        values = []
        for point in data:
            try:
                values.append((float(point["state"]), point["time"]))
            except (ValueError, KeyError):
                continue

        if not values:
            return {}

        float_vals = [v[0] for v in values]
        min_val, min_ts = min(values, key=lambda x: x[0])
        max_val, max_ts = max(values, key=lambda x: x[0])
        avg_val = sum(float_vals) / len(float_vals)
        med_val = statistics.median(float_vals)

        return {
            "min": {"value": round(min_val, 4), "time": self._ts_to_str(min_ts)},
            "max": {"value": round(max_val, 4), "time": self._ts_to_str(max_ts)},
            "average": round(avg_val, 4),
            "median": round(med_val, 4),
            "total_points": len(float_vals),
            "basis": (
                "raw in-range samples (independent of any aggregation or "
                "truncation applied to 'data')"
            ),
        }

    def _aggregate_data(
        self,
        data: List[Dict],
        period: str,
        mode: str,
    ) -> List[Dict]:
        """
        Aggregates datapoints by period (hourly/daily/weekly/monthly).
        mode: average / minmax / firstlast
        """
        if period == "none" or mode == "none":
            return data

        buckets: Dict[str, List] = defaultdict(list)

        for point in data:
            try:
                ts = datetime.fromtimestamp(point["time"] / 1000, tz=self.tz)
            except Exception:
                continue

            if period == "hourly":
                key = ts.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:00:00")
            elif period == "daily":
                key = ts.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
            elif period == "weekly":
                key = f"{ts.isocalendar()[0]}-W{ts.isocalendar()[1]:02d}"
            elif period == "monthly":
                key = ts.strftime("%Y-%m")
            else:
                key = ts.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")

            buckets[key].append(point)

        result = []
        for key in sorted(buckets.keys()):
            group = buckets[key]
            floats = []
            for p in group:
                try:
                    floats.append(float(p["state"]))
                except (ValueError, KeyError):
                    continue

            if not floats:
                continue

            if mode == "average":
                result.append(
                    {
                        "time": key,
                        "state": str(round(sum(floats) / len(floats), 4)),
                    }
                )
            elif mode == "minmax":
                result.append(
                    {"time": key + "_min", "state": str(round(min(floats), 4))}
                )
                result.append(
                    {"time": key + "_max", "state": str(round(max(floats), 4))}
                )
            elif mode == "firstlast":
                result.append(
                    {"time": key + "_first", "state": str(round(floats[0], 4))}
                )
                result.append(
                    {"time": key + "_last", "state": str(round(floats[-1], 4))}
                )

        return result

    def _truncate_data(
        self,
        data: List[Dict],
        max_datapoints: int,
        strategy: str,
    ) -> List[Dict]:
        """
        Truncates data to max_datapoints using the given strategy.
        oldest  = keep newest N (drop oldest)
        newest  = keep oldest N (drop newest)
        middle  = keep first N/2 and last N/2 (drop middle)
        """
        if len(data) <= max_datapoints:
            return data

        if strategy == "oldest":
            return data[-max_datapoints:]
        elif strategy == "newest":
            return data[:max_datapoints]
        elif strategy == "middle":
            half = max_datapoints // 2
            return data[:half] + data[-(max_datapoints - half) :]
        else:
            return data[:max_datapoints]

    def _apply_changes_filter(self, data: List[Dict]) -> List[Dict]:
        """
        Removes consecutive duplicate states, keeping only state-change events.
        The first datapoint is always kept to preserve the initial state.
        Example: 1440x CLOSED -> 2x CLOSED (start + end boundary) if no changes occurred.
        Example: CLOSED, OPEN, CLOSED -> all 3 kept (each is a state change).
        """
        if not data:
            return data
        result = [data[0]]
        for point in data[1:]:
            if point.get("state") != result[-1].get("state"):
                result.append(point)
        return result

    def _parse_local_to_ms(self, dt_str: str) -> Optional[int]:
        """Parses a 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS' local-time string (the format the LLM
        passes for starttime/endtime) to epoch milliseconds in the configured
        timezone. Returns None if the string can't be parsed, in which case the
        caller simply skips boundary partitioning.
        """
        if not dt_str:
            return None
        try:
            dt = datetime.strptime(dt_str, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
            return int(dt.replace(tzinfo=self.tz).timestamp() * 1000)
        except (ValueError, TypeError):
            return None

    def _fetch_state_after_range(
        self, device_name: str, after_time: str, service_id: str = None
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Fetches the first known datapoint strictly after after_time.
        Used to provide the LLM with the trailing state so it can reason
        about what happened after the requested range ended.
        Returns a dict with 'time' and 'state', or None on failure.
        """
        try:
            params_after = {"starttime": after_time}
            if service_id:
                params_after["serviceId"] = service_id
            response = self.session.get(
                f"{self.get_base_url()}/persistence/items/{device_name}",
                headers=self.get_headers(),
                params=params_after,
                timeout=10,
            )
            if response.status_code not in [200, 202]:
                return None
            data = response.json().get("data", [])
            if not data:
                return None
            point = data[0]
            return {
                "time": self._ts_to_str(point["time"]),
                "state": point["state"],
            }
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f"Could not fetch state after range for {device_name}: {e}")
            return None

    def get_historical_data_for_openhab_device(
        self,
        device_name: str,
        max_datapoints: int,
        truncation_strategy: str,
        show_statistics: bool,
        apply_changes_filter: bool = True,
        starttime: str = None,
        endtime: str = None,
        aggregation_period: str = "none",
        aggregation_mode: str = "none",
        service_id: str = None,
    ) -> str:
        """
        Core persistence query with aggregation, truncation and statistics.
        Processing order:
          1. Fetch all data from OpenHAB (boundary=true: OpenHAB also returns one
             datapoint before starttime and one after endtime)
          1.5 Split off the boundary datapoints so they neither pollute the
              statistics nor inflate the in-range count; the carry-in value is
              surfaced separately as 'state_before_range'
          2. Compute statistics (in-range dataset)
          3. Apply aggregation
          3.5 Apply changes filter (remove consecutive duplicate states)
          4. Apply truncation if still over limit
          4.5 Trailing anchor (from the boundary point, else a fallback query)
          5. Build result with truncation / changes-filter hint if needed
        """
        # Guard: reject unknown aggregation values with a descriptive error
        # instead of silently falling through (which previously yielded an
        # empty result the LLM could not recover from). Mirrors the vocabulary
        # reject so the model self-corrects in one step.
        valid_periods = {"none", "hourly", "daily", "weekly", "monthly"}
        valid_modes = {"none", "average", "minmax", "firstlast"}
        if aggregation_period not in valid_periods:
            return (
                f"Invalid aggregation_period: '{aggregation_period}'. "
                f"Use one of: {', '.join(sorted(valid_periods))}."
            )
        if aggregation_mode not in valid_modes:
            return (
                f"Invalid aggregation_mode: '{aggregation_mode}'. "
                f"Use one of: {', '.join(sorted(valid_modes))}."
            )
        try:
            params = {}
            if starttime:
                params["starttime"] = starttime
            if endtime:
                params["endtime"] = endtime
            if service_id:
                params["serviceId"] = service_id
            # boundary=true makes OpenHAB include the value valid at the range
            # edges: one point at/before starttime and one at/after endtime.
            # This gives the LLM the carry-in state for event-based persistence
            # (e.g. a switch/contact that did not change inside the window).
            if starttime or endtime:
                params["boundary"] = "true"

            response = self.session.get(
                f"{self.get_base_url()}/persistence/items/{device_name}",
                headers=self.get_headers(),
                params=params,
                timeout=15,
            )

            if response.status_code not in [200, 202]:
                error_msg = (
                    f"OpenHAB API error {response.status_code}: {response.text[:200]}"
                )
                logger.error(error_msg)
                return f"No Data: {error_msg}"

            try:
                raw = response.json()
            except json.JSONDecodeError:
                return "No Data: OpenHAB returned invalid JSON"

            all_data = raw.get("data", [])

            # Step 1.5 — Split off boundary datapoints (boundary=true).
            # OpenHAB returns the carry-in value (≤ starttime) and the trailing
            # value (≥ endtime) alongside the in-range data. Separate them so the
            # statistics and the in-range count stay clean, and expose the
            # carry-in value as 'state_before_range'.
            state_before_range = None
            boundary_after = None
            start_ms = self._parse_local_to_ms(starttime) if starttime else None
            end_ms = self._parse_local_to_ms(endtime) if endtime else None

            if start_ms is not None or end_ms is not None:
                in_range = []
                before_t = None
                after_t = None
                for p in all_data:
                    t = p.get("time")
                    if not isinstance(t, (int, float)):
                        in_range.append(p)
                        continue
                    if start_ms is not None and t < start_ms:
                        # keep the most recent point before the window
                        if before_t is None or t > before_t:
                            before_t = t
                            state_before_range = {
                                "time": self._ts_to_str(t),
                                "state": p["state"],
                            }
                    elif end_ms is not None and t > end_ms:
                        # keep the earliest point after the window
                        if after_t is None or t < after_t:
                            after_t = t
                            boundary_after = {
                                "time": self._ts_to_str(t),
                                "state": p["state"],
                            }
                    else:
                        in_range.append(p)
                all_data = in_range

            total_available = len(all_data)

            if total_available == 0:
                result_obj = {
                    "name": device_name,
                    "timezone": self._tz_label(),
                    "datapoints": "0",
                    "total_datapoints_available": "0",
                    "data": [],
                }
                if state_before_range:
                    result_obj["state_before_range"] = state_before_range
                    result_obj["note"] = (
                        "No state changes within the requested range. The item "
                        "held the state shown in 'state_before_range' for the "
                        "entire period."
                    )
                else:
                    result_obj["note"] = (
                        "No data available for the requested time range."
                    )
                return json.dumps(result_obj)

            # Step 2 — Statistics on the in-range dataset
            stats = {}
            if show_statistics:
                stats = self._compute_statistics(all_data)

            # Step 3 — Aggregation
            processed_data = all_data
            aggregation_applied = "none"
            if aggregation_period != "none" and aggregation_mode != "none":
                processed_data = self._aggregate_data(
                    all_data, aggregation_period, aggregation_mode
                )
                aggregation_applied = f"{aggregation_period}/{aggregation_mode}"
                logger.info(
                    f"Aggregation {aggregation_applied}: {total_available} -> {len(processed_data)} points"
                )

            # Step 3.5 — Changes filter (remove consecutive duplicate states)
            # Only meaningful on raw data — aggregated buckets represent time periods,
            # identical values are still valid distinct intervals (e.g. roller shutter at 100%).
            changes_filter_applied = False
            changes_filter_reduced = 0
            if apply_changes_filter and aggregation_period == "none":
                before_filter = len(processed_data)
                processed_data = self._apply_changes_filter(processed_data)
                changes_filter_reduced = before_filter - len(processed_data)
                if changes_filter_reduced > 0:
                    changes_filter_applied = True
                    logger.info(
                        f"Changes filter: {before_filter} -> {len(processed_data)} points "
                        f"({changes_filter_reduced} redundant states removed)"
                    )

            # Step 3.6 — Convert raw timestamps to local time strings
            # Aggregated data already uses string keys from _aggregate_data.
            # Raw data still has integer millisecond timestamps — convert here
            # so the LLM always sees human-readable local time, never Unix-ms.
            if aggregation_period == "none":
                processed_data = [
                    {
                        "time": self._ts_to_str(point["time"]),
                        "state": self._fmt_state(point["state"]),
                    }
                    for point in processed_data
                ]

            # Step 4 — Truncation
            truncated = False
            truncation_note = ""
            if len(processed_data) > max_datapoints:
                truncated = True
                processed_data = self._truncate_data(
                    processed_data, max_datapoints, truncation_strategy
                )
                truncation_note = (
                    f"\n\n[INFO FOR LLM] Data was truncated using strategy '{truncation_strategy}'. "
                    f"Showing {len(processed_data)} of {total_available} available datapoints "
                    f"(aggregation applied: {aggregation_applied}"
                    + (
                        f", changes filter removed {changes_filter_reduced} redundant states"
                        if changes_filter_applied
                        else ""
                    )
                    + f"). "
                    f"Statistics are calculated from the COMPLETE dataset regardless of truncation. "
                    f"Inform the user and suggest: adjust the time range, "
                    f"use aggregation, or ask admin to increase max_datapoints in settings. "
                    f"Do NOT call this function again automatically."
                )

            # Step 4.5 — Trailing anchor: first known state after endtime.
            # Prefer the boundary point OpenHAB already returned. Fall back to an
            # explicit query ONLY for a past endtime — querying a future
            # starttime makes OpenHAB hand back a stale value from before the
            # window (observed: a 'state after range' timestamped a day earlier).
            now_ms = int(time.time() * 1000)
            endtime_in_future = end_ms is not None and end_ms > now_ms

            trailing_state = boundary_after
            if trailing_state is None and endtime and not endtime_in_future:
                trailing_state = self._fetch_state_after_range(
                    device_name, endtime, service_id=service_id
                )

            # Validate: a "state after range" must actually lie after the data we
            # returned. Boundary clamping or a future-endtime fallback can yield a
            # point at/before the window — drop it so the LLM never sees a
            # nonsensical trailing anchor.
            if trailing_state is not None:
                anchor_ms = self._parse_local_to_ms(trailing_state.get("time"))
                last_in_range_ms = (
                    all_data[-1]["time"]
                    if all_data and isinstance(all_data[-1].get("time"), (int, float))
                    else None
                )
                if anchor_ms is not None:
                    if (
                        last_in_range_ms is not None and anchor_ms <= last_in_range_ms
                    ) or (end_ms is not None and anchor_ms <= end_ms):
                        trailing_state = None

            # Step 5 — Build result
            result_obj = {
                "name": device_name,
                "timezone": self._tz_label(),
                "datapoints": str(len(processed_data)),
                "total_datapoints_available": str(total_available),
                "aggregation": aggregation_applied,
                "changes_filter": (
                    f"removed {changes_filter_reduced} redundant states"
                    if changes_filter_applied
                    else "none"
                ),
                "truncation_strategy": truncation_strategy if truncated else "none",
                "data": processed_data,
            }

            if state_before_range:
                result_obj["state_before_range"] = state_before_range

            if trailing_state:
                result_obj["first_state_after_range"] = trailing_state

            if show_statistics and stats:
                result_obj["statistics"] = stats

            result = json.dumps(result_obj)

            if truncated:
                result += truncation_note
            elif changes_filter_applied:
                # Without this note the LLM tends to read a lower 'datapoints'
                # value (vs total_datapoints_available) as "data is missing" and
                # invents hidden earlier/later points. Make completeness explicit.
                result += (
                    f"\n\n[INFO FOR LLM] The changes filter removed "
                    f"{changes_filter_reduced} consecutive-duplicate state(s). "
                    f"The {len(processed_data)} returned datapoints are the "
                    f"COMPLETE set of state changes in the requested range. "
                    f"'total_datapoints_available' counts raw samples including "
                    f"those duplicates, so a lower 'datapoints' value does NOT "
                    f"mean data is missing or hidden — do NOT assume additional "
                    f"earlier or later datapoints exist beyond those shown."
                )

            if show_statistics and stats and aggregation_applied != "none":
                # The statistics are computed on the raw in-range samples, but
                # 'data' shows aggregated buckets. Without this note the LLM
                # treats both as the same series and reports contradictory
                # extremes (raw max 28.32 in stats vs 27.18 as the top hourly
                # average in data). Spell out which to use for what.
                result += (
                    f"\n\n[INFO FOR LLM] 'statistics' (min/max/avg/median) are "
                    f"computed from the RAW in-range samples, not from the "
                    f"aggregated values in 'data'. The true min/max can therefore "
                    f"exceed the highest/lowest aggregated bucket shown (a raw peak "
                    f"can fall between two buckets). Report extremes from "
                    f"'statistics'; describe the trend from 'data'."
                )

            if endtime_in_future:
                # The requested window extends past 'now'. Tell the LLM the data
                # is complete up to the present so it doesn't misread the missing
                # 'first_state_after_range' as a failure or wait for more data.
                result += (
                    f"\n\n[INFO FOR LLM] The requested endtime ({endtime}) is in "
                    f"the future — persistence only holds data up to the present "
                    f"moment. The last datapoint shown is the most recent one "
                    f"available; there is no later data and no 'state after range' "
                    f"yet. Treat the series as complete up to now."
                )

            logger.info(
                f"Persistence {device_name}: {total_available} in-range -> "
                f"{len(processed_data)} returned"
            )
            return result

        except requests.exceptions.Timeout:
            return f"No Data: Timeout connecting to OpenHAB ({self.host}:{self.port})"
        except requests.exceptions.ConnectionError:
            return f"No Data: Connection error to OpenHAB ({self.host}:{self.port})"
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Error getting persistence data for {device_name}: {e}")
            return f"No Data: {str(e)}"


class Tools:
    class Valves(BaseModel):
        openhab_host: str = Field(
            default="192.168.1.100", description="OpenHAB server IP address"
        )
        openhab_port: str = Field(default="8080", description="OpenHAB server port")
        openhab_api_token: str = Field(
            default="", description="OpenHAB API token (optional)"
        )
        max_search_results: int = Field(
            default=50, description="Maximum number of search results"
        )
        cache_ttl: int = Field(
            default=3600, description="Vocabulary cache TTL in seconds"
        )
        enable_item_filter: bool = Field(
            default=True, description="Enable item type filter"
        )
        filter_group_items: bool = Field(
            default=True, description="Filter out group items"
        )
        custom_filtered_types: str = Field(
            default="", description="Additional item types to filter (comma-separated)"
        )
        # --- Persistence settings (Admin only) ---
        max_datapoints: int = Field(
            default=200,
            description=(
                "Hard limit: maximum datapoints returned per persistence query. "
                "Increase for local LLM setups. Keep low for cloud APIs to protect token costs. "
                "The LLM controls aggregation — this is only a safety net."
            ),
        )
        truncation_strategy: str = Field(
            default="middle",
            description=(
                "Which data to keep when max_datapoints is exceeded. "
                "'newest' = keep oldest data, drop newest. "
                "'oldest' = keep newest data, drop oldest. "
                "'middle' = keep first and last half, drop middle."
            ),
        )
        show_statistics: bool = Field(
            default=True,
            description=(
                "Always append statistics (min/max/avg/median with timestamps) "
                "calculated from the FULL dataset before any truncation or aggregation. "
                "Recommended: keep enabled — allows accurate analysis even with truncated data."
            ),
        )
        apply_changes_filter: bool = Field(
            default=True,
            description=(
                "Remove consecutive duplicate states before truncation. "
                "Example: 1440 identical CLOSED entries become 1 entry — "
                "the LLM can infer nothing changed between them. "
                "Highly recommended for binary sensors (contact, motion, switch). "
                "Disable only if every individual datapoint timestamp matters."
            ),
        )
        timezone: str = Field(
            default="Europe/Berlin",
            description=(
                "Timezone for timestamp display and aggregation buckets. "
                "Must match the timezone used in starttime/endtime parameters. "
                "Examples: Europe/Berlin, Europe/London, America/New_York. "
                "See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones"
            ),
        )
        default_persistence_service: str = Field(
            default="jdbc",
            description=(
                "Default persistence service for historical queries. "
                "'jdbc' = PostgreSQL/MariaDB (recommended, event-based, no data loss). "
                "'rrd4j' = legacy RRD4J (minute-interval snapshots, long history). "
                "The LLM can override this per query using the service_id parameter."
            ),
        )
        enable_persistence_cache: bool = Field(
            default=True,
            description=(
                "Show historical data availability per item in search results. "
                "Queries jdbc and rrd4j for each item in the background (~13s on first load) "
                "and displays 'History: jdbc YYYY-MM-DD→YYYY-MM-DD | rrd4j ...' per result. "
                "Disable if you only use one persistence service or want faster initial load."
            ),
        )
        persistence_cache_services: str = Field(
            default="jdbc,rrd4j",
            description=(
                "Comma-separated list of persistence services to include in the availability cache. "
                "Only relevant when enable_persistence_cache is enabled. "
                "Examples: 'jdbc' (only jdbc), 'rrd4j' (only rrd4j), 'jdbc,rrd4j' (both)."
            ),
        )

    def __init__(self):
        self.valves = self.Valves()
        self.helper: Optional[OpenHABHelper] = None

    def search_openhab_items(self, query_words: List[str], mode: str = "and") -> str:
        """
        Main search function for OpenHAB items with controllability info

        Args:
            query_words: List of search terms from the vocabulary
            mode: "and" for precise search, "or" for broad search

        Returns:
            str: Formatted search results with controllability information
        """
        start_time = time.time()
        self.helper = OpenHABHelper.from_valves(self.valves, self.helper)
        helper = self.helper

        normalized_words = []
        umlaut_changes = []

        for word in query_words:
            normalized = helper.normalize_umlauts(word)
            normalized_words.append(normalized)
            if normalized != word:
                umlaut_changes.append(f"'{word}' → '{normalized}'")

        validation = helper.validate_query(normalized_words, mode)
        if not validation["valid"]:
            error_msg = validation["error"]
            if "complete_vocabulary" in validation:
                vocabulary_list = validation["complete_vocabulary"]
                error_msg += f"\n\n**All {len(vocabulary_list)} available keywords:**\n"
                error_msg += ", ".join(vocabulary_list)
                return error_msg
            return error_msg

        valid_words = validation["valid_words"]

        if mode.lower() == "and":
            results = helper.search_items_and(valid_words)
        else:
            results = helper.search_items_or(valid_words)

        search_time = round((time.time() - start_time) * 1000, 1)

        output = ""
        if umlaut_changes:
            output += f"Umlaut normalization: {', '.join(umlaut_changes)}\n"

        output += f"Search: {', '.join(valid_words)} ({mode.upper()} mode)\n"
        output += f"Time: {search_time}ms\n\n"
        output += helper.format_search_results(
            results, mode, self.valves.max_search_results
        )

        return output

    def historical_data_from_openhab_device(
        self,
        device_name: str,
        starttime: str = None,
        endtime: str = None,
        aggregation_period: str = "none",
        aggregation_mode: str = "none",
        service_id: str = None,
    ) -> str:
        """
        Returns historical persistence data for an OpenHAB device.

        RULES - follow exactly, no exceptions:

        1. ALWAYS search for the exact item name first via search_openhab_items.
           Use the FULL item name (e.g. 'steckdose_arbeitszimmer_schreibtisch_verbrauch_total').

        2. Datetime: pass exactly as given by the user, format 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS'.
           Do NOT add timezone suffixes (no Z, no +00:00).

        3. ALWAYS choose aggregation based on time range and item type:

           Time range -> aggregation_period:
             1 day          -> none
             2 to 7 days    -> hourly
             1 to 4 weeks   -> daily
             1 to 6 months  -> weekly
             more than 6 months -> monthly

           Item type -> aggregation_mode:
             cumulative (verbrauch_total, energy, counter) -> firstlast
             sensor data (temperature, humidity, pressure)  -> average
             power/current (leistung, strom, scheinleistung) -> average
             unknown                                         -> average

        4. Statistics (min/max/avg/median) are always from the COMPLETE dataset.
           For cumulative items: consumption = statistics.max.value - statistics.min.value
           For sensor data: use statistics.average

        5. If result contains [INFO FOR LLM]: data was truncated.
           Inform the user. Suggest shorter time range or finer aggregation.
           Do NOT call this function again automatically.

        6. PERSISTENCE SERVICE SELECTION:
           Default service is set via the valve 'default_persistence_service' (usually 'jdbc').
           Override with service_id when needed:
           - service_id='jdbc'  : current data, event-based, precise (recommended)
           - service_id='rrd4j' : historical data older than jdbc start date,
                                  minute-interval snapshots, long history available
           If the user asks about data from before jdbc was set up, use service_id='rrd4j'.

        Args:
            device_name:        Full OpenHAB item name (from search_openhab_items)
            starttime:          Start datetime, format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS
            endtime:            End datetime, format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS
            aggregation_period: none / hourly / daily / weekly / monthly
            aggregation_mode:   none / average / minmax / firstlast
            service_id:         Persistence service override: 'jdbc' or 'rrd4j' (optional)
        """
        self.helper = OpenHABHelper.from_valves(self.valves, self.helper)
        helper = self.helper
        effective_service_id = (
            service_id or self.valves.default_persistence_service or None
        )
        return helper.get_historical_data_for_openhab_device(
            device_name=device_name,
            starttime=starttime,
            endtime=endtime,
            max_datapoints=self.valves.max_datapoints,
            truncation_strategy=self.valves.truncation_strategy,
            apply_changes_filter=self.valves.apply_changes_filter,
            aggregation_period=aggregation_period,
            aggregation_mode=aggregation_mode,
            show_statistics=self.valves.show_statistics,
            service_id=effective_service_id,
        )

    def control_openhab_device(self, device_name: str, command: str) -> str:
        """Controls an OpenHAB device"""
        self.helper = OpenHABHelper.from_valves(self.valves, self.helper)
        helper = self.helper
        success = helper.send_command(device_name, command)

        if success:
            return f"[SUCCESS] {device_name} successfully set to '{command}'!"
        else:
            return f"[ERROR] Control of '{device_name}' failed"

    def get_vocabulary_list(self) -> str:
        """Shows all available keywords"""
        self.helper = OpenHABHelper.from_valves(self.valves, self.helper)
        helper = self.helper
        vocabulary = helper.get_vocabulary()
        vocabulary_list = sorted(list(vocabulary))

        output = f"Available keywords ({len(vocabulary_list)} total)\n\n"
        output += ", ".join(vocabulary_list)

        if helper.filter_enabled and helper.filtered_types:
            output += f"\n\nFilter active: {', '.join(helper.filtered_types)} excluded"

        return output

    def get_controllable_devices_summary(self) -> str:
        """Overview of all controllable devices"""
        self.helper = OpenHABHelper.from_valves(self.valves, self.helper)
        helper = self.helper
        items = helper.load_openhab_items()

        if not items:
            return f"[ERROR] Items could not be loaded: {helper.last_load_error}"

        controllable_items = [
            item for item in items if helper.is_controllable_item(item)
        ]
        readonly_items = [
            item for item in items if not helper.is_controllable_item(item)
        ]

        output = f"Smart Home Overview\n\n"
        output += f"Total: {len(items)} devices\n"
        output += f"Controllable: {len(controllable_items)} devices\n"
        output += f"Status only: {len(readonly_items)} devices\n\n"

        type_groups = {}
        for item in controllable_items:
            item_type = item.get("type", "Unknown")
            if item_type not in type_groups:
                type_groups[item_type] = []
            type_groups[item_type].append(item)

        output += "Controllable devices by type:\n"
        for item_type, items_of_type in sorted(type_groups.items()):
            commands = helper.get_possible_commands(items_of_type[0])
            output += (
                f"- {item_type}: {len(items_of_type)} devices ({', '.join(commands)})\n"
            )

        output += "\nTip: Use the search function to find specific devices!"

        output += helper.persistence_overview_text()

        return output

specs.name: historical_data_from_openhab_device
specs.description:
Returns historical persistence data for an OpenHAB device.

RULES - follow exactly, no exceptions:

1. ALWAYS search for the exact item name first via search_openhab_items.
Use the FULL item name (e.g. 'steckdose_arbeitszimmer_schreibtisch_verbrauch_total').

2. Datetime: pass exactly as given by the user, format 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS'.
Do NOT add timezone suffixes (no Z, no +00:00).

3. ALWAYS choose aggregation based on time range and item type:

Time range -> aggregation_period:
1 day          -> none
2 to 7 days    -> hourly
1 to 4 weeks   -> daily
1 to 6 months  -> weekly
more than 6 months -> monthly

Item type -> aggregation_mode:
cumulative (verbrauch_total, energy, counter) -> firstlast
sensor data (temperature, humidity, pressure)  -> average
power/current (leistung, strom, scheinleistung) -> average
unknown                                         -> average

4. Statistics (min/max/avg/median) are always from the COMPLETE dataset.
For cumulative items: consumption = statistics.max.value - statistics.min.value
For sensor data: use statistics.average

5. If result contains [INFO FOR LLM]: data was truncated.
Inform the user. Suggest shorter time range or finer aggregation.
Do NOT call this function again automatically.

6. PERSISTENCE SERVICE SELECTION:
Default service is set via the valve 'default_persistence_service' (usually 'jdbc').
Override with service_id when needed:
- service_id='jdbc'  : current data, event-based, precise (recommended)
- service_id='rrd4j' : historical data older than jdbc start date,
minute-interval snapshots, long history available
If the user asks about data from before jdbc was set up, use service_id='rrd4j'.

Args:
device_name:        Full OpenHAB item name (from search_openhab_items)
starttime:          Start datetime, format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS
endtime:            End datetime, format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS
aggregation_period: none / hourly / daily / weekly / monthly
aggregation_mode:   none / average / minmax / firstlast
service_id:         Persistence service override: 'jdbc' or 'rrd4j' (optional)

specs.name: search_openhab_items
specs.description:
Main search function for OpenHAB items with controllability info

Args:
query_words: List of search terms from the vocabulary
mode: "and" for precise search, "or" for broad search

Returns:
str: Formatted search results with controllability information
